question: How to create a modal dialog in HTML
description: The native <dialog> element provides accessible modals with built-in focus trapping, Escape key support, and a ::backdrop pseudo-element — no library needed.

steps.title: Create the <dialog> element
steps.explanation: Add a <dialog> element anywhere in your HTML. It is hidden by default. Give it an id so you can reference it from JavaScript.
steps.code:
Confirm action

Are you sure you want to proceed?

steps.title: Open and close with showModal() and close()
steps.explanation: showModal() opens the dialog as a true modal — it traps focus inside and closes automatically when the user presses Escape. close() dismisses it.

steps.title: Close on backdrop click
steps.explanation: Clicking the backdrop does not close the modal by default. Check that the click target is the <dialog> element itself (not its children) to dismiss on outside-click.

steps.title: Style the backdrop with ::backdrop
steps.explanation: The ::backdrop pseudo-element covers the page behind the dialog. You can blur or dim it freely with CSS.
